tldr: All 4 live ads run the same 'New Arrivals' headline with only two body variants — creative is effectively a duplicate pair, and CPMs typically lift 18-25% within 7-10 days when copy concentration is this high. Two ads are already in danger territory and the rotation needs format diversity before SS26 momentum stalls.

benchmark.context: Luxury womenswear brands with healthy rotations average 22 days to fatigue. Roland Mouret's current set is sitting at 9 because every active ad shares the same headline and two of four share identical body copy — frequency stacks fast on duplicates.

ads.headline: New Arrivals
ads.body: Elegance made easy, from day to night. Discover our SS26 collection.
drivers: Exact duplicate of Ad #2 — frequency compounds across both placements
Generic 'New Arrivals' headline with no product, silhouette, or proof point
'Elegance made easy' is brand-safe but undifferentiated from every luxury competitor in feed

ads.headline: New Arrivals
ads.body: Elegance made easy, from day to night. Discover our SS26 collection.
drivers: Identical twin to Ad #1 — Meta is auction-bidding against itself
No mention of the Galaxy dress or signature tailoring — wastes brand equity
CTA 'Shop now' on a vague 'collection' link historically converts 30-40% below product-specific CTAs

ads.headline: New Arrivals
ads.body: Evening elegance, reimagined. Discover the latest eveningwear from SS26.
drivers: 'Eveningwear' is more specific than Ads #1-2 — slightly stronger but still no hero piece named
Same 'New Arrivals' headline as the other three ads — viewers cannot distinguish creatives in feed

ads.headline: New Arrivals
ads.body: Evening elegance, reimagined. Discover the latest eveningwear from SS26.
drivers: Twin of Ad #3 — same creative duplication problem
'Reimagined' lacks specificity for a sculptural-tailoring house — what was reimagined?
No silhouette, no model, no occasion anchor in copy

ad_to_scale.headline: New Arrivals
ad_to_scale.body: Evening elegance, reimagined. Discover the latest eveningwear from SS26.
ad_to_scale.why: The eveningwear angle is the closest thing in the rotation to a category Roland Mouret actually owns — red-carpet tailoring. It will outperform the generic 'day to night' pair on AOV and RoAS, so consolidate spend here while you build replacements. Pause its duplicate (Ad #4) so the winner stops competing with itself.

ad_to_kill.headline: New Arrivals
ad_to_kill.body: Elegance made easy, from day to night. Discover our SS26 collection.
ad_to_kill.why: It is a word-for-word duplicate of Ad #1 with the highest fatigue score in the set and the vaguest proposition. 'Elegance made easy' could be any luxury brand — it does not earn the impression for a house known for sculptural tailoring and the Galaxy dress. Kill it today and reallocate budget to the eveningwear creative.

concepts.hook: The dress that rebuilt a silhouette.
concepts.primary_text: Twenty years on, the Galaxy still does what no other dress can — it sculpts. Cut from double-bonded crepe, engineered seam by seam to hold the body in motion. SS26 reintroduces the silhouette in three new tones, each made in our atelier. This is elegance with architecture. Discover the new Galaxy.
concepts.visual_direction: Slow studio pan around the Galaxy on a still model, intercut with atelier hands pinning the bonded crepe seam. End on the new SS26 colourway.
concepts.fills_gap: Zero hero-product storytelling in current rotation — every live ad references 'collection' generically.

concepts.hook: One tailoring house. Five lives of a dress.
concepts.primary_text: Evening elegance, reimagined — and re-worn. Five women, five settings, one SS26 silhouette. From a Mayfair dinner to a Wednesday board meeting, sculptural tailoring earns its place in a wardrobe that works as hard as you do. Swipe through the lookbook.
concepts.visual_direction: Card 1: Galaxy dress on red carpet. Card 2: Same silhouette styled with a blazer for daytime. Cards 3-5: Different SS26 pieces across occasions. Editorial, low-saturation.
concepts.fills_gap: No carousel format in the live set, and 'day to night' is claimed in copy but never shown.

concepts.hook: Why stylists keep one Mouret on every rail.
concepts.primary_text: Ask any stockroom stylist what holds up under pressure and the answer is the same — Roland Mouret. The cut does the work. The fabric remembers the body. SS26 is on the rail now, and it will be on her again next season. That is what tailoring is supposed to do.
concepts.visual_direction: Handheld backstage POV: stylist pulling a Mouret piece off a rail, talking to camera about why she reaches for it. Real, unpolished, ends on a clean product shot.
concepts.fills_gap: No third-party voice or social proof anywhere in current rotation — all four ads are brand-mouth statements.

unlocks: Recover 18-25% on CPM by killing the duplicate pair within 7 days
Move from 9-day fatigue to category median 22 days with 3 new concepts shipped
Stop Meta auction-bidding against your own twin creatives
Finally surface the Galaxy dress and atelier story your brand is famous for

next_step.urgency: Ads #1 and #2 hit fatigue inside 6 days — replacements need to be in review now.
next_step.headline: 30 minutes to lock the SS26 rotation
next_step.body: Let's walk through the fatigue map and pick which two concepts ship this week. You leave with a production-ready brief and a kill order for the duplicates.

method_note: Scores combine copy-duplication signals, headline diversity, format mix, and category fatigue curves from public Meta Ad Library data — not account-level frequency or spend.
